title: Post 2I Japan
wikitext:
[]: Post 2I Japan             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Thu Jul 21, 2005 7:24 pm Just a little question: Has anybody ever tried to find out if there are any significative differences between the maps and images of post 2I Japan they show in Eva with actual Japan?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 21, 2005 7:32 pm The 'satellite shots' in EoE definitely show, from what I can tell, a severe reduction of land area. I've looked at a couple of other maps associated with NGE (the production drawing and what's provided in the RPG book), and the reduction is questionably meager. I haven't been able to get ahold of detailed enough elevation maps of Japan to be certain, but I'm pretty sure more of Japan would have been swallowed by the rise of the ocean than what is depicted in those two sources. Overall, it's really hard to make an educated determination one way or another. Maybe I'm just 1337 enough at cartography.

[]:             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Fri Jul 22, 2005 11:50 am yeah its all flooded, and in the ep with israfel after they use the n2 mine you see a giant chunch of land missing on the map.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jul 22, 2005 11:52 am That's because it made a big hole in the ground, as N^2 mines are apt to do... Doesn't have anything to do with the rising of the oceans and consumption of the coastlines that resulted from the Antarctic ice cap melting.
